jokes.text: Uncle Phil, you really would have buried Judge Robertson... if you would have let me put the Uh-Huh Girls in there with you. It would have been like, 'Banks is the right one, baby, uh-huh!'
jokes.setup: Will giving advice about Uncle Phil's political campaign commercial
jokes.punchline: Suggesting adding backup dancers called 'Uh-Huh Girls' to a judicial campaign ad
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Will's street sensibility clashing with upper-class political norms. The specific 'Uh-Huh Girls' reference is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Philip Banks, and I'm running for Superior Court Judge.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil's serious judicial campaign commercial
jokes.punchline: Cut to inappropriate home movie footage from New Year's Eve party
jokes.explanation: Classic contrast between formal political messaging and embarrassing reality. The visual comedy is well-constructed.

jokes.text: And if you put me on the bench, I'll take a real bite out of crime. Bite.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil trying to deliver a serious law-and-order message
jokes.punchline: Cut to him literally biting into food at the party, followed by 'Bite' caption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual pun that turns a clichéd political slogan into literal action. The economy is perfect - maximum funny per word.

jokes.text: Vote for Philip Banks. Philip Banks.
jokes.setup: Standard political ad closing
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's voice saying his name over embarrassing party footage
jokes.explanation: Solid payoff to the commercial sequence, though less inspired than the earlier beats.

jokes.text: Emmy!
jokes.setup: Will seeing Uncle Phil's reaction to the disastrous commercial
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically suggesting the ad is Emmy-worthy
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical response that shows Will's obliviousness to how badly he's messed up. One perfect word.

jokes.text: Oh, please, from who? From Latisha. She said you looked really handsome.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil sarcastically asking who gave positive feedback about the commercial
jokes.punchline: Will's innocent response citing his girlfriend's opinion
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Will missing the point entirely. Sets up the next joke nicely.

jokes.text: But she also thought Delta Burke looked better as a blonde.
jokes.setup: Will defending the positive feedback from Latisha
jokes.punchline: Revealing Latisha's questionable judgment with pop culture reference
jokes.explanation: Clever undermining of the 'positive' feedback by showing Latisha's questionable taste. Delta Burke reference dates it but works for the era.

jokes.text: Trevor, that's so sweet. But, honey, call me Mrs. Banks.
jokes.setup: Trevor complimenting Vivian on looking blessed
jokes.punchline: Vivian politely but firmly establishing boundaries
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Vivian's class and Trevor's overfamiliarity. Mild but effective.

jokes.text: Home of the Whopper, what's your beef?
jokes.setup: Will answering the phone
jokes.punchline: Using Burger King slogan as phone greeting with 'beef' pun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will character moment - casual, streetwise, clever wordplay. Shows his personality in one line.

jokes.text: Some chicken here talking about she from the Oprah Winfrey Show.
jokes.setup: Will dismissing the caller
jokes.punchline: Calling the Oprah show booker a 'chicken' and using dismissive tone
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Will's skepticism and street vernacular. Sets up the surprise that it's legitimate.

jokes.text: This is Trevor Collins, goodbye and Godspeed.
jokes.setup: Trevor finishing a phone call with his Oprah contact
jokes.punchline: Overly formal, pretentious sign-off for a casual call
jokes.explanation: Perfect Trevor characterization - pretentious, formal, trying too hard to sound important. 'Godspeed' is hilariously overwrought.

jokes.text: Well, actually, Phil, she doesn't, you're nobody.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking why Oprah would care about them
jokes.punchline: Trevor's brutally honest assessment delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery of harsh truth. The casual way Trevor deflates Phil's ego is perfectly executed and very quotable.

jokes.text: What was it? God.
jokes.setup: Family asking about Alexis's cute nickname for Trevor
jokes.punchline: Trevor revealing she called him 'God'
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure. One word that completely subverts expectations and reveals Trevor's ego. Maximum economy and impact.

jokes.text: Wait, am I going to need a passport?
jokes.setup: Family excited about going to Chicago
jokes.punchline: Ashley thinking she needs a passport for domestic travel
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ashley's sheltered Bel-Air upbringing. Simple but effective geography humor.

jokes.text: And I think I speak for everyone in this room when I say: 'What am I going to wear?'
jokes.setup: Carlton expressing disappointment that Will can't go
jokes.punchline: Hilary claiming to speak for everyone but only caring about her outfit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - self-absorbed, tone-deaf to the situation, making everything about her appearance. Well-structured misdirection.

jokes.text: And as soon as you tell him, let me know.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil saying they need to tell Will he can't go
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately deflecting the responsibility after saying it needs to be done
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Phil's cowardice about confronting Will. Sets up the ongoing avoidance pattern.

jokes.text: How difficult could it possibly be?
jokes.setup: Phil saying he couldn't tell Will the bad news
jokes.punchline: Vivian's confident assertion right before we see how difficult it will be
jokes.explanation: Classic 'famous last words' setup that the audience knows will be immediately contradicted. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Have we met?
jokes.setup: Will appears dressed to fit in with the family
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's sarcastic reaction to Will's formal transformation
jokes.explanation: Sharp reaction to the visual gag of Will's makeover. Phil's sarcasm cuts through Will's attempt to transform.

jokes.text: Well, that's telling him, Philip.
jokes.setup: Phil completely failing to tell Will he can't go
jokes.punchline: Vivian's sarcastic comment on Phil's cowardice
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the setup about how difficult it could be. Vivian's sarcasm lands well.

jokes.text: Wow, I'm sorry for staring, but... man, girl, you real fine. You know, I was a little nervous about flying, but... as long as I can look at you, I know I'll be all right.
jokes.setup: Flight attendant trying to talk to Will professionally
jokes.punchline: Will completely misreading the situation and flirting awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Typical Will behavior but feels a bit forced and dated. The setup works but execution is clunky.

jokes.text: Can I fluff your pillow?
jokes.setup: Will continuing to misread the flight attendant's professional interaction
jokes.punchline: Innocent-sounding request with obvious double meaning
jokes.explanation: Cheap innuendo humor that doesn't add much. Economy is decent but relies on obvious double entendre.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, I'll get you in one later.
jokes.setup: Family taking group photo at Oprah's studio
jokes.punchline: Will casually excluding Uncle Phil from the photo
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Will's casual dismissal of Phil. The irony works given their earlier dynamic.

jokes.text: Honey, couldn't we just put him to sleep?
jokes.setup: Family struggling with how to handle the Will situation
jokes.punchline: Vivian suggesting euthanizing Will like a pet
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and unexpected from the usually maternal Vivian. Perfect deadpan delivery of an absurd solution.

jokes.text: You have beautiful hair. I know.
jokes.setup: Alexis complimenting Hilary
jokes.punchline: Hilary's matter-of-fact acceptance without gratitude
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character moment. Her casual vanity and lack of social grace delivered with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Well, I tell you what, if you really like it, I will send you one in your size.
jokes.setup: Hilary complimenting Oprah's suit
jokes.punchline: Oprah's offer implying Hilary would need a much larger size
jokes.explanation: Body size humor that's dated and somewhat mean-spirited. Works for the era but feels cheap now.

jokes.text: Gee, Oprah, I really like that diamond bracelet. Nice try.
jokes.setup: Will seeing Oprah's generosity to Hilary
jokes.punchline: Will immediately trying to capitalize, Oprah shutting him down
jokes.explanation: Great setup/punchline showing Will's opportunistic nature and Oprah's sharp response. Well-constructed exchange.

jokes.text: Okay, everybody who's going to be on the Oprah Show, take one step forward. Not so fast, Will.
jokes.setup: Phil finally having to tell Will he can't be on the show
jokes.punchline: The military-style lineup followed by Will being singled out
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the long setup of Phil avoiding telling Will. The military format makes the exclusion more dramatic.

jokes.text: I've always wanted my own gavel.
jokes.setup: Oprah asking Phil about his serious reasons for wanting to be a judge
jokes.punchline: Phil admitting his motivation is wanting a gavel like a toy
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Phil's childlike side beneath his serious exterior. Perfectly honest and endearing.

jokes.text: We're not ready for that portion of the show just yet, but, Mrs. Banks... I'm Will, Oprah, I can answer it.
jokes.setup: Oprah trying to maintain show structure
jokes.punchline: Will immediately interrupting and correcting her
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Will's inability to follow TV protocol. Sets up the escalating chaos.

jokes.text: No, Philip, it's Richard Nixon wearing a Will mask.
jokes.setup: Phil asking if that's really Will on TV
jokes.punchline: Vivian's sarcastic response with absurd imagery
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic response with vivid absurd imagery. The Nixon reference adds historical weight to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Well, I sure would, son. And let me say, I love your Leslie Uggams.
jokes.setup: Will asking the Senator about bringing his nephew on TV
jokes.punchline: Senator confusing Will's appearance with 1970s performer Leslie Uggams
jokes.explanation: Good generational/cultural confusion joke. Leslie Uggams reference is specific and unexpected enough to work.

jokes.text: You are so brave. I mean, wearing red shoes with a pink dress.
jokes.setup: Response to woman sharing recovery story
jokes.punchline: Hilary commenting on fashion instead of personal struggle
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and character work. Hilary's priorities are hilariously skewed - she's 'brave' for fashion choices, not life struggles.

jokes.text: Has being in politics ruined your sex life? No, mine's great, how's yours, honey?
jokes.setup: Inappropriate personal question to Phil
jokes.punchline: Phil deflecting with confident response and turning it back
jokes.explanation: Solid comeback showing Phil's confidence and quick thinking. Good economy in the response.

jokes.text: Sit down, shut up, and behave yourself.
jokes.setup: Phil trying to maintain dignity on national TV
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown into parental authoritarianism
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's complete loss of composure and political image on live TV

jokes.text: You all are related?
jokes.setup: Watching the family dysfunction unfold
jokes.punchline: Oprah's shocked realization about the family connection
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that acknowledges how dysfunctional the family appears. Oprah as audience surrogate works well.

jokes.text: Here, you poor sweetheart. Blow. You poor baby.
jokes.setup: Will appears to be crying in the audience
jokes.punchline: Oprah treats him like a child, offering tissues
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Oprah's maternal instincts toward Will

jokes.text: Don't you think so, audience? Go on up there and be with your family, where you belong.
jokes.setup: Will's emotional plea about feeling excluded
jokes.punchline: Oprah rallying audience support to override the family's decision
jokes.explanation: Good use of Oprah's real persona and show format. The emotional manipulation aspect rings true to her style.

jokes.text: Oprah, this guy can't even run his own home. Why should he be elected to office?
jokes.setup: Will now on stage with the family
jokes.punchline: Will immediately attacking Uncle Phil's candidacy based on home life
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's revenge. Links personal family drama to political competence effectively.

jokes.text: I mean, when I lost my job and he kicked me out of the house... he let me live out back.
jokes.setup: Hilary defending her father as a good guy
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing he kicked her out of the house
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and misdirection. Hilary thinks she's defending Phil but actually makes him look worse.

jokes.text: Well, he didn't actually kick her out of the house. He just changed all the locks and told her she didn't live there anymore.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to defend his father's actions
jokes.punchline: Making the technical distinction that somehow makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation through Carlton's earnest attempt to help. The technical distinction makes Phil look even worse.

jokes.text: Well, with the big pay cut you'll have to take, we'll all be living in the pool house.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Hilary living in the pool house
jokes.punchline: Vivian revealing the family's financial concerns about Phil's career change
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the escalating family dysfunction. Adds economic reality to the political fantasy.

jokes.text: Look, I don't like to tell tales out of school, but Trevor keeps a make-up case there.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Trevor staying in the pool house
jokes.punchline: Carlton revealing suspicious detail about Trevor's belongings
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that adds to the family chaos. Carlton's innocent delivery of suspicious information works well.

jokes.text: Stand on a chair and say that to my face.
jokes.setup: Carlton calling Trevor a liar
jokes.punchline: Trevor's threat that acknowledges Carlton's short stature
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy reference and threat that's both menacing and acknowledges Carlton's height. Economical and effective.

jokes.text: You have both lost your American Express cards. Do you want to work on your Visa?
jokes.setup: Hilary and Carlton arguing on television
jokes.punchline: Phil threatening to take away their credit cards in ascending order
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating threat structure that shows Phil's parenting style. The credit card hierarchy is perfectly wealthy-family specific.

jokes.text: Well, if you ask me your whole damn family is crazy.
jokes.setup: Watching the Banks family dysfunction unfold
jokes.punchline: Audience member stating the obvious truth
jokes.explanation: Simple truth-telling that works as audience surrogate. Not sophisticated but effectively sums up the situation.

jokes.text: Hey, man, don't nobody talk about my mother, man.
jokes.setup: Audience member insulting Will's mother
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate defensive reaction crossing into physical confrontation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's loyalty limits. The physical escalation works as payoff to the building tension.

jokes.text: You know, your initials, O. W? Did anybody ever tell you that spells 'ow'?
jokes.setup: Will talking to Oprah after the show
jokes.punchline: Noticing her initials spell a word of pain
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that shows Will's observational nature. Not groundbreaking but character-appropriate and mildly clever.

jokes.text: I knew that.
jokes.setup: Will thinking he's clever about her initials
jokes.punchline: Oprah's flat response deflating his observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of Will's attempt to be clever. Economy is excellent - two words that deflate his entire observation.

jokes.text: Two free tickets to the Donahue Show. Tell Phil Oprah said, 'Hi.'
jokes.setup: Oprah giving Will something as consolation
jokes.punchline: Giving him tickets to her competitor's show with a message
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about talk show rivalry. The casual way she sends Phil to her competitor is nicely petty.

jokes.text: She likes me.
jokes.setup: Oprah sending Phil to her rival's show
jokes.punchline: Will completely missing that this is an insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's ability to find positive spin in any situation. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: Will, you're smiling like Dad does when he finishes the Happy Birthday bucket at Baskin-Robbins.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Will's satisfied expression
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to Uncle Phil's ice cream indulgence smile
jokes.explanation: Great specific comparison that reveals character details about both Will and Phil. The 'Happy Birthday bucket' detail is perfectly specific.

jokes.text: Man, you scream your girl's name out on TV, you'd be smiling, too.
jokes.setup: Carlton commenting on Will's satisfaction
jokes.punchline: Will revealing he accomplished his goal of shouting out Latisha
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Will's earlier promise to Latisha. Shows his priorities and provides closure to that plot thread.

jokes.text: And in a related story, Judge Carl Robertson announced today that he is adopting 12 ghetto youths.
jokes.setup: Will suggesting Phil's humanity would win him the election
jokes.punchline: His opponent immediately one-upping with extreme version
jokes.explanation: Excellent political satire showing how politicians cynically one-up each other. The specific number and phrasing make it perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Oh, well.
jokes.setup: Learning his opponent has completely out-maneuvered him
jokes.punchline: Phil's casual acceptance of political defeat
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated response to complete political defeat. Phil's resigned acceptance is both funny and endearing. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: Oh, God! You have just made the blooper reel.
jokes.setup: Phil repeatedly flubbing his line during credits
jokes.punchline: Hilary pointing out he's creating bloopers
jokes.explanation: Meta-reference to TV production that provides closure to the episode's theme of Phil's media troubles. Functional ending.
